D9 BiologyL5 EvaluateReference

GFP Chromophore — β-Barrel Encapsulation

GFP's chromophore (p-HBDI) forms spontaneously from three amino acids inside a rigid 11-strand β-barrel; the barrel suppresses non-radiative decay → bright 509 nm fluorescence

GFP from Aequorea victoria has a Ser-Tyr-Gly tripeptide that spontaneously cyclises into the p-HBDI chromophore — entirely encoded in the gene. Without the protein barrel the chromophore is non-fluorescent in solution (rapidly relaxes via twisting). The β-barrel cage suppresses cis-trans isomerisation; the chromophore fluoresces strongly. This auto-formation + auto-folding makes GFP a fusable, encodable fluorescent tag — Nobel 2008 Shimomura/Chalfie/Tsien.

Toggle barrel/no-barrel to see fluorescence on/off.
